title: EPIC: Full unit + E2E test coverage (no mocks)
description:
Achieve comprehensive unit and E2E integration test coverage across the oc-blackbytes plugin without mocks, fakes, stubs, or spies. Use real filesystem temp dirs, real spawned processes, and real plugin bootstrapping. Existing discipline: tests use bun:test with temp dirs and OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR env override for isolation — extend this pattern.

Current state: 38 tests, 6 files, ~87 source files. Major gaps: hashline-edit (17 files, 0 tests), ast-grep, grep, glob, shared tool helpers, MCP factories, most agent/handler code, bootstrap wiring, setup-models command, and any E2E pipeline.

Success = every production source file has direct or compositional coverage; E2E runners exercise the real plugin against a temp OpenCode config dir with greeting banners and detailed step-level logging; CI enforces a coverage threshold.
delete_reason: Replacing existing graph with OpenCode built-in LSP upgrade plan

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text:
## Execution order (dependency spine)

1. **EPIC A** (harness) — unblocks everything. Must land first. 6 leaves.
2. **EPICS B–L** (unit tests) — all depend on A; largely parallel. Recommended priority within:
   - First: E (hashline-edit, 17 files untested, most leverage). Leaves E1/E2/E3/E4/E5/E8/E9/E14 are P0.
   - Then: L4 (tool-execute-after read path, P0), I2 (spawn, P0).
   - Then: everything else, P1.
3. **EPIC M** (E2E harness) — depends on A3 (plugin harness) + A5 (logger) + A6 (fixtures). Can start in parallel with unit-test epics once A is done.
4. **EPIC N** (E2E scenarios) — depends on M1. N5 (hashline roundtrip) is P0 because it catches integration regressions across the most complex subsystem.
5. **EPIC O** (coverage gates + CI) — runs last; O2 depends on M1.

## Parallelism sketch
After A: fire B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, L simultaneously (disjoint file targets).
After M: fire all N scenarios in parallel (disjoint scenario files).

## Discipline (no-mock policy)
- **No** jest/vitest-style mocks, spies, stubs, fakes.
- Real temp dirs, real spawned processes, real local HTTP server for download tests (I4).
- Binary-dependent tests (sg, rg, find) detect availability via A4 and skip cleanly.
- Test-doubles that ARE allowed: concrete classes with deterministic behavior (e.g., TestClient in A3) — these are real implementations, not mocks. Document this in the file header.

title: A. Test harness foundation
description: Shared test utilities for real-filesystem, real-process testing. No mocks. Provides: temp dir helpers (auto-cleanup), OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR orchestration, plugin bootstrap harness, spawn capture helpers, binary-availability probes (to skip cleanly when sg/grep/find are absent), test fixture config files. Everything downstream depends on this.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text:
Foundation epic — every other epic (B–M) has a blocking edge to A. A1/A2/A3/A5/A6 are critical; A4 is safety net for missing-binary skips.

Implementation order: A1 → A2 (parallel with A6) → A3 → A5 → A4.

title: test/helpers/tmp-dir.ts — temp directory harness
description:
## Objective
Create test/helpers/tmp-dir.ts exporting makeTmpDir(prefix?: string): { path: string; cleanup: () => Promise<void> } that uses fs.mkdtempSync under os.tmpdir() and registers afterEach/afterAll-safe cleanup.

## Context
All downstream tests (unit + E2E) need a uniform, race-safe temp dir primitive. The project's testing discipline already uses real temp dirs (see test/config.test.ts and test/workspace-boundary.test.ts). This centralises that pattern.

## Scope
IN: makeTmpDir helper, writeFixture(path, content) helper, writeJsoncFixture helper, directory tree builder helper (buildTree({ 'a.txt': 'x', 'sub/b.txt': 'y' })).
OUT: no mocking frameworks, no in-memory fs.

## Components touched
- test/helpers/tmp-dir.ts (new)
- test/helpers/index.ts (new; re-exports)

## Dependencies / Prerequisites
None.

## Constraints
- Must use node:fs and node:os, not bun-specific APIs, to match the existing test style.
- cleanup must be idempotent and best-effort (never throw).

## DoD
- New helper unit-tested itself in test/helpers.test.ts with a round-trip buildTree → read back.
- bun test passes.
- bun run check passes.
delete_reason: Replacing existing graph with OpenCode built-in LSP upgrade plan

title: CI: GitHub Actions (or chosen CI) run test + test:e2e + coverage
description:
## Objective
Ensure CI runs the full gate on push + PR.

## Components
- .github/workflows/test.yml (inspect the repo first: if a workflow already exists, extend it; otherwise create this file)

## Required behavior
- Triggers: on push to any branch and on pull_request to default branch.
- Job: ubuntu-latest.
- Steps, in order: actions/checkout@v4; setup-bun (oven-sh/setup-bun@v2 with bun-version from package.json engines if set, else latest); bun install --frozen-lockfile (or bun install if no lockfile committed); bun run check; bun test --coverage; bun run test:e2e.
- Artifacts: upload test/e2e/artifacts/** on failure so NDJSON logs are inspectable.
- Coverage thresholds are enforced by O1 (bunfig.toml); if they fail, the 'bun test --coverage' step fails the job.

## DoD (locally verifiable)
- Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/test.yml.
- YAML parses (run 'yamllint' or equivalent if installed; otherwise 'bun run check' is sufficient since the file is not in src/test scope).
- File contains the six ordered steps above and the artifact upload.
- 'actions/checkout@v4' and 'oven-sh/setup-bun@v2' pinned to their major tag.

title: OpenCode built-in LSP upgrade
description:
Upgrade oc-blackbytes to support OpenCode's experimental built-in core `lsp` tool safely through documentation, prompt guidance, runtime-context wording, a guidance-first setup command, and regression tests. The upgrade must not implement or register a custom LSP client/tool.

## Success Criteria
- Agents use conditional LSP-first/fallback guidance without claiming `oc-blackbytes` registers `lsp`.
- Users have docs and a `/setup-lsp` command explaining env flags, `permission.lsp`, and OpenCode LSP config.
- Runtime context distinguishes oc-blackbytes-managed resources from OpenCode core tools.
- Regression tests cover prompts, command registration/override behavior, and runtime context semantics.
- Implementation validation commands are captured in leaf beads.
close_reason: Completed OpenCode built-in LSP upgrade epic

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE] All child beads for the OpenCode built-in LSP upgrade are complete. Delivered canonical docs, conditional Bytes/Explore/General prompt guidance, runtime-context wording, setup-lsp command, regression tests, validation evidence, and post-MVP gap analysis. Remaining check failures are documented pre-existing ast-grep sg ENOENT plus MCP unused-import warnings.

title: Verify OpenCode built-in LSP capability facts
description:
## Objective
Verify the target OpenCode built-in `lsp` tool facts before any docs, prompts, or command template hard-code behavior.

## Context
`oc-blackbytes` should use OpenCode's core experimental `lsp` tool as an ambient tool when available. Current working assumptions from prior research: tool name `lsp`; env flags `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TOOL=true` or `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL=true`; permission key `permission.lsp = "allow"`; operations include `goToDefinition`, `findReferences`, `hover`, `documentSymbol`, `workspaceSymbol`, `goToImplementation`, `prepareCallHierarchy`, `incomingCalls`, and `outgoingCalls`; current docs describe `line` and `character` as 1-based editor-style positions. Re-check before downstream wording.

## Scope
- Verify current OpenCode docs/source or live schema for env flags, permission key, operation names, argument names, coordinate convention, and config examples.
- Record concise canonical facts in project docs or implementation notes that downstream docs/prompts/command can cite.
- Avoid adding custom LSP implementation.

## Components Touched
- `docs/configuration.md` or a focused docs page/section for recorded facts.
- No runtime code unless existing docs location requires index/link updates.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- None. This is the prerequisite for all LSP wording work.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- Use Context7/current OpenCode docs/source or live runtime schema; do not rely only on memory.
- Do not query or include secrets in documentation lookups.
- If facts cannot be verified, document uncertainty and avoid coordinate-specific examples downstream.

## Risks/Gotchas
- OpenCode LSP is experimental and may change operation names or schema.
- Plugin API currently should not be assumed to expose a stable LSP registration/control API.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Verified facts cover env flags, permission key, operation names, argument names, coordinate convention, and OpenCode config examples.
- Downstream docs/prompts can reuse this note without re-researching.
- The note clearly states `lsp` is OpenCode core, not a plugin-bundled tool.
design: Use current OpenCode documentation/source as the source of truth. Prefer Context7 docs lookup for OpenCode/anomalyco opencode; if using source, cite the exact file/path or URL in the docs note. Record the verification date. This bead should not change prompt code, command code, or bundled tool registration.
acceptance_criteria: Verified facts are recorded in a durable docs/notes location and cover: env flags, permission key, operation names, argument names, coordinate convention, and config examples. The note states that core lsp is owned by OpenCode, not oc-blackbytes. No custom LSP client/tool implementation is added.
close_reason: Implemented: recorded verified OpenCode core LSP facts for downstream docs/prompts/commands

Acceptance emphasis: facts must cover env flags, permission key, operation names, argument names, coordinate convention, and config examples; note must state core lsp is OpenCode-owned, not oc-blackbytes-owned.

title: Capture post-MVP custom LSP gap analysis
description:
## Objective
Capture deferred gaps between OpenCode's built-in `lsp` tool and possible future custom plugin LSP tools so future work is deliberate.

## Context
MVP intentionally avoids custom LSP JSON-RPC clients and custom tools like diagnostics or semantic rename. After built-in LSP is exercised or smoke-tested, record concrete gaps instead of speculating.

## Scope
- Add a short docs/planning section or follow-up note listing gaps vs oh-my-openagent capabilities.
- Track potential gaps: callable diagnostics, `prepareRename`/semantic rename, WorkspaceEdit preview/apply behavior with hashline expectations, server install UX.
- Define decision criteria for when custom plugin LSP tools would be worth building.

## Components Touched
- `docs/plans/opencode-builtin-lsp-upgrade.md`, a follow-up docs file, or Beads comments/notes depending on project convention.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on validation/smoke evidence when available.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- This is documentation/planning only.
- Do not implement a custom LSP client or custom LSP tools in this bead.
- MVP completion does not depend on this bead unless smoke validation produces concrete findings that must be recorded immediately.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Avoid designing future tools before observing actual built-in limitations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A concise follow-up gap list exists with evidence level clearly marked.
- Decision criteria for future custom LSP work are documented.
- No custom LSP implementation is added.
close_reason: Documented post-MVP custom LSP gaps and future decision criteria

Cover diagnostics, prepareRename/rename, WorkspaceEdit/hashline implications, and server install UX.

title: Document OpenCode built-in LSP setup
description:
## Objective
Add user-facing documentation for enabling and using OpenCode's experimental built-in `lsp` tool with oc-blackbytes.

## Context
Users need a safe setup path for OpenCode core LSP support. The plugin should explain how to enable core `lsp`, but must not imply `oc-blackbytes` registers or disables it. LSP settings belong in OpenCode config (`opencode.json` / possibly `opencode.jsonc`), not `oc-blackbytes.jsonc`.

## Scope
- Update `docs/configuration.md` or add a focused docs page/section.
- Explain env flags: `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TOOL=true` and `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL=true`.
- Include permission snippet using `permission.lsp = "allow"`.
- Include basic `lsp` server config example with `command` and `extensions`.
- Describe limitations, fallbacks, and the boundary between OpenCode core tools and oc-blackbytes bundled tools.

## Components Touched
- `docs/configuration.md` and/or another docs file linked from existing docs/README if appropriate.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on verified OpenCode LSP capability facts.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- Do not add `lsp` to plugin bundled tool lists.
- Do not add OpenCode core LSP settings to `oc-blackbytes.jsonc` schema.
- Env flags must be set before OpenCode starts; the plugin cannot enable them late for the current process.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Avoid overpromising: not every workspace has a working language server.
- Keep docs version-tolerant because the tool is experimental.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docs clearly distinguish OpenCode core `lsp` from plugin-bundled tools (`hashline_edit`, `ast_grep_search`, `ast_grep_replace`, `grep`, `glob`).
- Docs include env flag, permission, and basic custom server examples.
- Docs instruct users to configure OpenCode config files, not `oc-blackbytes.jsonc`.
- Docs mention fallback to existing search/read workflows when `lsp` is unavailable or unsupported.
close_reason: Implemented: documented OpenCode core LSP setup and fallbacks

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Implementation guidance: Keep docs user-facing and setup-oriented. Recommended section shape: what OpenCode core lsp is; how to enable env flag before launch; permission.lsp snippet; optional lsp server snippet; limitations/fallbacks; boundary with oc-blackbytes disabled_tools. Do not document oc-blackbytes.jsonc as the place for core LSP settings.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH PASS 2] Readiness clarification: Treat ocb-tte.1 as the canonical source for exact LSP facts. If verification changes any currently assumed env flag, permission key, operation name, argument name, coordinate convention, or config shape, update this docs bead's examples to match the verified facts rather than preserving the current draft wording.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [IMPLEMENTATION] Expanded docs/configuration.md OpenCode core LSP section with user setup path, env restart note, permission and server snippets, OpenCode-config-not-oc-blackbytes boundary, limitations, and fallback guidance. Validation: bun run build passed.

title: Clarify runtime context resource semantics
description:
## Objective
Update `<available_resources>` wording so it describes oc-blackbytes-managed resources rather than the complete OpenCode runtime tool inventory.

## Context
`src/extensions/agents/utils/runtime-context.ts` currently says agents should only reference tools/MCPs/agents listed in `<available_resources>`. That conflicts with OpenCode core tools such as `read`, `write`, `bash`, and experimental `lsp`, because `lsp` is not registered by the plugin and must not be listed in plugin `enabledTools`.

## Scope
- Update runtime context copy to identify listed tools as oc-blackbytes-managed bundled tools.
- Preserve existing behavior of listing plugin-bundled tools, MCP servers, and peer agents.
- Do not add `lsp` to `enabledTools` or plugin bundled tool constants.
- If adding any static note, keep it clear that OpenCode core tools are governed by runtime tool availability and permissions.

## Components Touched
- `src/extensions/agents/utils/runtime-context.ts`
- `test/agents/runtime-context.test.ts` or equivalent existing runtime context tests.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on verified OpenCode LSP capability facts.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- `enabledTools` means plugin-bundled tools only.
- `disabled_tools` semantics remain limited to plugin-bundled tools.
- This task is wording/semantics only; no core tool discovery system is required.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Do not weaken guidance so much that agents hallucinate unavailable plugin resources.
- Keep distinction between plugin MCP servers, plugin tools, peer agents, and OpenCode core tools explicit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runtime context no longer says agents may only reference tools listed in `<available_resources>`.
- Runtime context still accurately lists enabled oc-blackbytes-managed resources.
- Tests assert `lsp` is not listed as a bundled tool and wording does not imply complete runtime inventory.
close_reason: Implemented: clarified available_resources semantics and tests

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Implementation guidance: Change wording in buildRuntimeContextSection() only as needed. Describe listed resources as oc-blackbytes-managed resources and avoid treating the list as a complete OpenCode runtime inventory. Do not add core tool discovery and do not add lsp to enabledTools/BUNDLED_TOOLS. Tests should assert lsp is not bundled and the 'only reference listed resources' wording is gone.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [IMPLEMENTATION] Updated runtime-context wording to describe oc-blackbytes-managed bundled tools/MCPs/peer agents without claiming the section is a complete OpenCode runtime inventory. Did not add lsp to bundled tools. Added focused runtime-context regression coverage. Validation: bun test test/agents/runtime-context.test.ts passed; bun run build passed.

title: Add conditional LSP workflow to Bytes prompts
description:
## Objective
Teach Bytes prompt variants to use OpenCode core `lsp` for semantic code intelligence when available, while falling back to existing bundled tools.

## Context
Bytes has model-aware prompts under `src/extensions/agents/bytes/`. The LSP section must be compact and conditional so agents do not call unavailable tools or waste context on simple searches.

## Scope
- Add a concise shared `lspWorkflow` section in `src/extensions/agents/bytes/agent.ts` or an equivalent prompt helper.
- Inject it into `default.ts`, `gpt.ts`, and `gemini.ts` in the appropriate prompt location.
- Guidance should map semantic needs to operations: definitions (`goToDefinition`), references (`findReferences`), hover/type context (`hover`), outline (`documentSymbol`), workspace symbols (`workspaceSymbol`), call hierarchy when available.
- Include fallback to `grep`, `glob`, `ast_grep_search`, and `read` when `lsp` is missing, fails, or no language server is configured.

## Components Touched
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/agent.ts`
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/default.ts`
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/gpt.ts`
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/gemini.ts`
- Prompt tests in `test/agents/bytes-agent.test.ts` or equivalent.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on verified LSP capability facts.
- Should align with runtime context wording changes.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- Always phrase as conditional: “When the `lsp` tool is available…”.
- Never claim `oc-blackbytes` registers `lsp`.
- Keep section compact; current prompts are already substantial.
- Do not use LSP for simple filename/text searches where `glob`/`grep` is cheaper.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Prompt bloat and over-delegating to LSP can slow simple tasks.
- Coordinate convention should not be hard-coded unless verified.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All Bytes prompt variants include conditional LSP guidance and fallback rules.
- Prompt tests assert desired wording without brittle full-prompt snapshots.
- No Bytes prompt lists `lsp` as a bundled oc-blackbytes tool.
close_reason: Implemented conditional LSP workflow for Bytes prompt variants

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Implementation guidance: Implement the LSP prompt as a compact reusable section. Put operation mapping in prose, not a long manual. Suggested core line: 'When the OpenCode core lsp tool is available, use it for semantic definitions/references/hover/symbols; if unavailable or inconclusive, immediately fall back to grep/glob/ast_grep_search/read.' Keep model-specific prompt style intact.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH PASS 3] Scope boundary: This bead owns adding Bytes prompt guidance and any minimal adjacent test updates needed to keep the changed prompt code safe. The broader cross-agent/runtime regression matrix is owned by ocb-tte.7; do not duplicate a large prompt-test suite here.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [IMPLEMENTATION START] Starting Bytes prompt LSP workflow bead after user confirmed the 5-file plan.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [IMPLEMENTATION] Added shared Bytes LSP workflow guidance and injected it into default, GPT, and Gemini prompt variants. The wording treats lsp as an OpenCode core tool, maps semantic needs to verified operations, discourages LSP for cheap text/file search, and falls back to grep/glob/ast_grep_search/read. Added targeted Bytes prompt assertions. Validation: bun test test/agents/bytes-agent.test.ts (26 pass); bun run build (passed).

title: Update Explore and General LSP guidance
description:
## Objective
Correct subagent prompt guidance so Explore and General use OpenCode core `lsp` conditionally for semantic code understanding.

## Context
Explore currently references generic “LSP tools” despite no plugin-provided LSP tools. General should also know when semantic LSP operations are useful, while retaining normal search/read workflows.

## Scope
- Update `src/extensions/agents/explore.ts` so it no longer implies unconditional LSP tools exist.
- Update `src/extensions/agents/general.ts` with compact conditional LSP guidance for definitions/references/hover/symbols/call hierarchy when available.
- Ensure both agents fallback immediately to `grep`, `glob`, `ast_grep_search`, and `read` when LSP is unavailable or unhelpful.

## Components Touched
- `src/extensions/agents/explore.ts`
- `src/extensions/agents/general.ts`
- `test/agents/builtin-agents.test.ts` or equivalent prompt tests.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on verified LSP capability facts.
- Should align with Bytes prompt wording and runtime context semantics.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- Explore remains read-only; do not grant write/edit permissions.
- General remains an implementation executor; LSP guidance must not add a custom LSP dependency.
- Avoid worsening Explore's tendency to over-fan-out; LSP should be used when semantically valuable, not as mandatory first action.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Existing tests may assert prompt substrings; update them intentionally.
- Do not mention unavailable oh-my-openagent-specific tool names like `lsp_diagnostics` or `lsp_rename` as current tools.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Explore prompt uses conditional wording for OpenCode core `lsp` and no longer has unconditional generic “LSP tools” mismatch.
- General prompt includes semantic LSP guidance and fallback behavior.
- Tests cover presence of desired guidance and absence of known-bad unconditional wording.
close_reason: Implemented conditional OpenCode core lsp prompt guidance for Explore and General

For General, add concise semantic-navigation guidance without changing permissions or suggesting custom lsp_diagnostics/lsp_rename tools. Preserve fallback to existing search/read tools.

Added builtin agent regression coverage for Explore plus default/GPT General prompts. Validation: bun test test/agents/builtin-agents.test.ts (27 pass); bun run build (passed).

title: Add setup-lsp built-in command template
description:
## Objective
Add a built-in `/setup-lsp` command that guides users through enabling OpenCode's experimental built-in `lsp` tool safely.

## Context
Commands are static `CommandDefinition.template` strings. `/setup-lsp` must instruct Bytes through a safe workflow; it is not an imperative config editor and should not introduce a new command execution subsystem.

## Scope
- Create `src/extensions/commands/setup-lsp.ts`.
- Export/register it from `src/extensions/commands/index.ts` via `createBuiltinCommands()`.
- Template should instruct Bytes to explain experimental status, locate OpenCode config via `OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR` then standard config dir such as `~/.config/opencode/`, inspect `opencode.jsonc` and `opencode.json`, ask if both exist, prefer advisory output, and edit only after explicit confirmation.
- Template should instruct JSONC-aware merging where possible and avoid lossy rewrites unless user accepts.
- Template should explain env flag must be set before restart/new process and optionally show LSP server config.

## Components Touched
- `src/extensions/commands/setup-lsp.ts` (new)
- `src/extensions/commands/index.ts`
- `src/extensions/commands/types.ts` only if needed by existing command style.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on verified LSP facts and setup docs wording.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- Target OpenCode config (`opencode.json` / `opencode.jsonc`), not `oc-blackbytes.jsonc`.
- Preserve existing `permission`, `lsp`, `agent`, `mcp`, and plugin config fields.
- Do not silently choose between `opencode.json` and `opencode.jsonc` if both are present.
- Do not promise enabling env vars in the already-running OpenCode process.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Safety is prompt-enforced; tests should assert template instructions rather than assume programmatic guarantees.
- Existing command handler preserves user-defined commands by skipping conflicts; do not break that behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `createBuiltinCommands()` includes `setup-lsp` and still includes `setup-models`.
- The command is a static template with clear safety instructions.
- No new config editor subsystem is introduced.
close_reason: Implemented setup-lsp command template and registration tests

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Implementation guidance: Follow the existing setup-models command pattern. The new file should export a CommandDefinition. The template should guide Bytes, not perform edits itself. Include key safety instructions for config discovery, explicit confirmation before edits, JSONC/comment preservation, env restart requirement, and OpenCode config target files.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH PASS 2] Readiness clarification: Keep setup-lsp aligned with the documentation from ocb-tte.2 and the canonical facts from ocb-tte.1. The template should avoid hard-coded coordinate examples unless ocb-tte.1 verifies them, and should explicitly say it is configuring OpenCode core LSP support rather than an oc-blackbytes tool.

Validation: bun test test/handlers/command-config-handler.test.ts (14 pass); bun run build (passed).

title: Add prompt and runtime context regression tests
description:
## Objective
Add regression tests proving agent prompts and runtime context handle OpenCode core `lsp` conditionally and honestly.

## Context
The upgrade changes prompt text and runtime context wording. Tests should assert meaningful substrings and absence of bad wording, not full snapshots.

## Scope
- Add/update Bytes prompt tests for default/GPT/Gemini variants.
- Add/update Explore prompt test for conditional LSP wording and absence of unconditional “LSP tools” assumptions.
- Add/update General prompt test for conditional semantic LSP guidance.
- Add/update runtime context test proving `lsp` is not listed as a bundled tool and `<available_resources>` no longer claims it is the complete runtime tool inventory.

## Components Touched
- `test/agents/bytes-agent.test.ts`
- `test/agents/builtin-agents.test.ts`
- `test/agents/runtime-context.test.ts`
- Agent prompt files only if test work reveals missing expected wording.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on runtime context wording change and prompt updates.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- Prefer semantic string assertions over brittle full-prompt snapshots.
- Test both presence of desired guidance and absence of known-bad wording.
- Do not require a live OpenCode LSP server in unit tests.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Prompt wording may be model-specific; tests should allow each variant's style while checking core behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Targeted prompt/runtime-context tests pass.
- Tests verify `lsp` is conditional and not represented as a plugin-bundled tool.
- Tests verify fallback guidance exists for missing/unavailable LSP.
close_reason: Implemented LSP prompt and runtime-context regression coverage

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Test guidance: Write focused substring tests, not full prompt snapshots. Assert conditional phrase, lsp operation names where appropriate, fallback tools, and absence of bad wording such as treating available_resources as complete runtime inventory. Tests must not require a live LSP server.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH PASS 3] Test ownership clarification: Treat this bead as the consolidation pass for regression coverage after ocb-tte.3/4/5 land. If those implementation beads added small local assertions, extend or normalize them here rather than creating duplicate brittle tests.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [IMPLEMENTATION] Consolidated regression coverage is in place across Bytes prompts, Explore/General built-in prompts, and runtime context tests. Assertions cover conditional OpenCode core lsp wording, operation/fallback guidance, absence of unconditional 'LSP tools' assumptions, and runtime context not treating lsp as a bundled/complete inventory item. Validation: bun test test/agents/bytes-agent.test.ts test/agents/builtin-agents.test.ts test/agents/runtime-context.test.ts (68 pass); latest bun run build passed after prompt changes.

title: Add setup-lsp command registration tests
description:
## Objective
Add/update command tests for the new built-in `setup-lsp` command and user override behavior.

## Context
Existing command tests may assume only `setup-models` exists. Adding `setup-lsp` should preserve `setup-models` and keep handler behavior that skips user-defined commands unchanged.

## Scope
- Update tests that assert exact built-in command count or only `setup-models`.
- Assert `createBuiltinCommands()` contains both `setup-models` and `setup-lsp`.
- Add command handler test proving a user-defined `command["setup-lsp"]` is preserved unchanged and the built-in is skipped.
- Add template-content assertions for safety instructions: ask before editing, target OpenCode config files, preserve comments/fields where possible, explain env flag restart requirement.

## Components Touched
- `test/handlers/command-config-handler.test.ts`
- Any command-specific tests if existing style uses a separate file.
- `src/extensions/commands/index.ts` / `setup-lsp.ts` only if tests reveal registration/content gaps.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on `setup-lsp` command template implementation.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- Command registration should not overwrite user-defined commands.
- Tests should not execute real config edits; the command is a static template.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Avoid asserting the entire template, which would make harmless wording edits noisy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Command tests pass with both built-ins present.
- User override test proves `command["setup-lsp"]` remains unchanged.
- Template-content tests cover the safety constraints from the plan.
close_reason: Implemented setup-lsp command registration and safety tests

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Test guidance: Update assumptions from one built-in command to multiple built-ins. Test command registration at the registry and handler layers. Template tests should check key safety phrases rather than exact full command text. Ensure user-defined command["setup-lsp"] is preserved unchanged.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [IMPLEMENTATION] Expanded command tests for setup-lsp registration: createBuiltinCommands contains setup-models/setup-lsp, handler registers both built-ins, setup-lsp user overrides are preserved, and template safety wording is asserted without full-template snapshots. Validation: bun test test/handlers/command-config-handler.test.ts (15 pass); bun run build (passed).

title: Run upgrade validation and capture smoke-test evidence
description:
## Objective
Validate the LSP upgrade with targeted tests, build/check commands, and manual smoke-test evidence when possible.

## Context
The implementation changes prompts, docs, runtime-context wording, and command registration. Full `bun run check` may expose a pre-existing environment-specific ast-grep binary issue; if so, document it separately rather than treating it as caused by this upgrade.

## Scope
- Run targeted tests: `bun test test/agents/bytes-agent.test.ts test/agents/builtin-agents.test.ts test/agents/runtime-context.test.ts test/handlers/command-config-handler.test.ts`.
- Run `bun run build`.
- Run `bun run check` or document unrelated pre-existing failures.
- When possible, manually smoke test OpenCode: start with `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TOOL=true`, set `permission.lsp = "allow"`, confirm `lsp` is visible, and run harmless `documentSymbol` or `hover` on a known source file.
- If smoke test cannot run, document blocker and substitute evidence.

## Components Touched
- No production code expected unless validation uncovers a defect.
- Test output and optional docs/plan note for manual smoke evidence.

## Dependencies/Prerequisites
- Depends on docs, prompt/runtime context changes, command implementation, and regression tests.

## Important Assumptions/Constraints
- Do not fix unrelated ast-grep binary reproducibility issues in this bead unless directly required by changed tests.
- Manual smoke testing depends on local OpenCode runtime availability.

## Risks/Gotchas
- Environment-specific failures must be clearly separated from implementation regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Targeted tests pass.
- `bun run build` passes.
- `bun run check` passes or unrelated known environment failure is documented with evidence.
- Manual smoke test result or inability to run it is recorded.
close_reason: Captured targeted validation, build/check results, and OpenCode LSP smoke evidence

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text: [POLISH] Validation guidance: Run after implementation beads are complete. Treat ast-grep binary failures as known environment issues only if the failure matches prior sg/binary-path symptoms; otherwise investigate normally. Record manual smoke test evidence in comments or docs if it cannot be automated.

comments.author: cuongnt3

Build passed: bun run build. Full check status: biome phase now has only pre-existing MCP unused-import warnings; unit phase still fails in test/ast-grep/cli.test.ts because sg binary execution returns ENOENT (same known environment issue observed before this work), 826 pass / 4 fail. Manual OpenCode LSP smoke: opencode --version => 1.14.22; OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TOOL=true opencode debug lsp document-symbols file:///home/cuongnt3/Work/personal/oc-blackbytes/src/index.ts returned [] without crashing; workspace symbols query also returned [] without crashing, likely no language server symbols configured in this non-interactive environment.
